item.title: 'Here to stay': Middle East upstream investment set to weather recent challenges
item.description: Close to $100 billion worth of greenfield projects are in the pipeline in the Middle East, while repair bills are already nearing $50 billion

item.title: Equinor eyes Angola exploration wells after 'deepening' portfolio
item.description: Norwegian player's head of exploration outlines drilling plans in Angola, Brazil and Argentina

item.title: German energy minister unwilling to let TotalEnergies return offshore wind sites
item.description: Katherina Reiche reportedly insists that bids entered are legally binding contracts as oil firms seek to give back several gigawatts of sites won in 2023-25

item.title: US-Iran peace deal reopens Strait of Hormuz and restarts Iranian oil sales
item.description: MoU between Iran and US sees quick oil sanctions relief but leaves future of Strait of Hormuz an open question

item.title: Norwegian operator takes FID on North Sea wells project
item.description: Balder Next New Wells project comprises a first-phase development of seven new wells

item.title: Equinor partnership agrees to concept on large cluster project
item.description: Ringvei Vest will commercialise seven discoveries in Norway's Troll-Fram area

item.title: BP lines up contractor for West of Shetland decommissioning gig
item.description: DeepOcean will support decommissioning and recycling of subsea equipment at UK supermajor’s Foinaven field

item.title: Sao Tome targets fast-track conclusion to licensing round
item.description: Head of petroleum agency tells Upstream production sharing contracts could be finalised by end of 2026

item.title: Rush of corporate deals in Norway as companies boost their positions
item.description: Vaar Energi leads deals with four other companies on the NCS

item.title: Major turning point for Vantris Energy
item.description: Former Sapura Energy exits Practice Note 17 status

item.title: EU set to miss 2030 CCS targets, says WoodMac
item.description: Fragmentation of value chain major stumbling block in scaling CCS capacity

item.title: Seismic sector eyes rebound as explorers widen search for new barrels
item.description: Oil supply shortfall by 2050, energy security concerns and surging crude prices among factors pointing to future increase in activity

item.title: GTT wins key gig for strategic China LNG project
item.description: French engineer to design ‘very large’ liquefied natural gas storage tanks

item.title: Timor-Leste's $20 billion-plus LNG project gathering steam
item.description: Dili to offer favourable terms for new PSC on the understanding Sunrise's gas lands on its soil

item.title: SLB lays out planned shift toward primary digital strategy to investors
item.description: SLB chief executive Olivier Le Peuch tells investors 'we are becoming a digital-first company'

item.title: Trump defends Iran deal, admits planned payouts to Tehran
item.description: The agreement frees traffic through the Strait of Hormuz and makes Tehran eligible for reconstruction payments

item.title: Turkey and Iraq at loggerheads over extension of oil pipeline agreement - report
item.description: Ankara insists a new agreement between the two governments has to replace the expiring oil export transit deal

item.title: IEA sees huge crude oil supply overhang in store for 2027
item.description: Big gap in oil supply vs. demand presents an opportunity to restock strategic reserves, IEA says

item.title: Former BP CEO Looney joins board of US fusion energy developer
item.description: Appointment is latest in string of new roles for Bernard Looney, who resigned from UK supermajor in 2023

item.title: Hungary's MOL steps closer to taking control of Serbian operator
item.description: MOL Group signs agreement with Serbian government on terms for managing NIS

item.title: Canadian independent ramps up Central Asia gas ambitions
item.description: Condor Energies chief executive Don Streu talks to Upstream about company's projects in Uzbekistan and Kazakhstan

item.title: Panoro wraps up Africa acquisition - paying $53 million less than agreed
item.description: Oil price surge due to Iran war sees Oslo-listed player pay Kosmos far less for Equatorial Guinea stake

item.title: UK by-election holds potential keys to North Sea's oil and gas future
item.description: While voters go to polls in Aberdeen on Thursday, it is electors in Greater Manchester who could shape future North Sea oil and gas policy

item.title: Ensco hit with tenth UK safety notice in five years
item.description: Two men have fallen to their deaths from Valaris rigs in the UK North Sea since 2023

item.title: Strait of Hormuz crisis sounds echoes of 1973
item.description: Impact of effective closure of Strait of Hormuz bears similarities with Opec oil embargo - but this time gas is a critical piece in the game

item.title: Cold feet: Why Big Oil shuns Alaska's Arctic National Wildlife Refuge
item.description: Reputational risk, potential litigation and expensive greenfield costs amid potential factors keeping major oil and gas companies away from latest oil and gas lease sale

item.title: ExxonMobil to supply LNG to South Africa in milestone deal
item.description: Supermajor signs heads of agreement with owners of planned import terminal in Richard's Bay

item.title: First laden oil tankers leave Iran as US blockade shows signs of softening
item.description: Iranian sources and vessel trackers suggest a growing number of vessels are going in and out of the Islamic Republic

item.title: Equinor outlines FID plans for next greenfield projects
item.description: Projects in Canada, Norway and Angola are planned for FID

item.title: Transocean wins contracts for two harsh environment semisubs
item.description: Awards add about $185 million to backlog
